items.title: A simple text fixture reused across many tests
items.scenario: Five tests all need 'a moderately long sample document'.
items.explanation: Define `sample_text` once in `conftest.py`; every test that names it as an argument receives it. Edit once when the dataset changes.

items.title: Mocking the LLM client
items.scenario: Your `summarize_doc(text)` calls OpenAI; you don't want test runs to cost money or fail on Wi-Fi.
items.explanation: Patch `ragapp.summarizer.openai_client.chat.completions.create` to return a fixed completion. Your test now runs in 2ms and never touches the network.

items.title: Faking the clock to test backoff
items.scenario: Backoff is 1s → 2s → 4s up to 60s. You don't want the test to actually wait.
items.explanation: Use `freezegun` or a `monkeypatch.setattr('time.sleep', lambda s: None)` plus injected clock. Assert the *sequence* of waits without burning seconds.

items.title: Temp-directory fixture for index files
items.scenario: Test exercises a vector store that writes index files to disk.
items.explanation: Use the built-in `tmp_path` fixture — pytest gives you a per-test temp dir and cleans it up. No risk of polluting the repo or cross-test interference.

items.title: Spy on logs
items.scenario: Test that a 429 produces a `WARNING` log line.
items.explanation: Use the built-in `caplog` fixture. `with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING): ...` then `assert any('429' in r.message for r in caplog.records)`.
